topic: An athlete doesn't make the team they expected to join
raw_output:
[story 1]

The weight of the acceptance letter felt like a physical blow. Liam stared at the glossy paper, the congratulatory stamp mocking the hollow ache in his chest. He had poured every waking hour into those grueling morning practices, every sacrifice etched into the calluses on his hands, all for this moment. Instead, the names on the roster were a different constellation entirely. He watched Sarah, whose effortless grace seemed to glide across the field, receiving the coveted spot with a casual nod from the coach. A tightness began to coil in Liam’s stomach, a knot of sharp, unwelcome energy. He forced a smile, the muscles in his face feeling stiff and unnatural, and mumbled something about being disappointed, the words tasting like ash. He turned away before anyone could see the slight tremor in his hand.

[story 2]

"Well, that's a shame, isn't it?" Marcus remarked, clapping Liam on the shoulder with a warmth that felt entirely misplaced. Liam managed a tight nod, his gaze fixed on the ground. He could feel the heat rising in his neck, a flush spreading across his cheeks that he desperately tried to cool down. He noticed how easily the others interacted, the shared laughter echoing around the group, a sound that seemed to bounce off him, landing flatly. He fiddled with the drawstring of his shorts, his fingers tracing the worn fabric with unnecessary intensity. *They* got the opportunity. It was unfair. He took a deep, shaky breath, trying to project an indifference he absolutely did not feel, and steered the conversation toward something mundane, his voice coming out a little too sharp, a little too brittle.

[story 3]

The locker room buzzed with the triumphant energy of the selected squad. Every glance, every shared inside joke, seemed calibrated to highlight the difference between them and the rest of the group. Daniel watched the team huddle, the camaraderie a tangible force pulling them together. A strange, hot pressure built behind his sternum. He watched the way the coach pointed, the easy camaraderie that seemed to flow naturally to those chosen. Daniel clenched his jaw so hard he could feel the strain in his temples. When it was his turn to speak, his voice cracked slightly on the opening sentence, a thin, reedy sound that immediately drew attention. He focused intently on a smudge on the floor, willing himself to become invisible, feeling every second of the shared moment stretching into an agonizing eternity.
